flashcards.a: A closed figure bounded by four line segments, having four sides, four angles, and four vertices.
A quadrilateral in which both pairs of opposite sides are strictly parallel.

flashcards.q: What effect does a diagonal have on a parallelogram?
flashcards.a: It perfectly divides the parallelogram into two congruent triangles.

flashcards.q: True or False: In a parallelogram, opposite sides and opposite angles are always equal.
If each pair of opposite sides of a quadrilateral is equal, what shape is it?
If the diagonals of a quadrilateral bisect each other, what shape is it?
Identify the parallelogram: Its internal angles are all 90 degrees.
Identify the parallelogram: All four of its sides are equal in length.
Identify the parallelogram: All sides are equal and all angles are 90 degrees.
What is unique about the diagonals of a rectangle?
flashcards.a: They bisect each other and are perfectly equal in length.

flashcards.q: What is unique about the diagonals of a rhombus?
flashcards.a: They bisect each other specifically at right angles (perpendicularly).

flashcards.q: What is unique about the diagonals of a square?
flashcards.a: They are exactly equal in length and bisect each other at right angles.

flashcards.q: What shape marks the intersection of the angle bisectors of a generic parallelogram?
According to the Mid-point Theorem, what is the relationship between the line segment joining the mid-points of two sides of a triangle and the third side?
flashcards.a: The line segment is parallel to the third side and exactly half of its length.

flashcards.q: A line is drawn through the exact mid-point of one side of a triangle, parallel to a second side. What does it do to the third side?
flashcards.a: It precisely bisects the third side.
